name: Tortelloni Di Zucca
recipeInstructions:
1. For Pasta: Combine flour, eggs, and oil in a mixer or processor with a dough hook. Add water as needed to form a soft ball. Knead on a lightly floured surface for about 5 minutes. Cover and let rest for 30 minutes.
2. For Filling: Halve the squash and remove seeds and strings. Cook in boiling salted water until tender (12 minutes). Drain, cool briefly, and scoop out flesh (about 2 cups). Steam escarole with water, then drain and chop coarsely. Set aside.
3. Melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add squash and cook until dry (5 minutes), breaking up the flesh. Stir in Parmesan cheese and parsley, lower heat to medium, and cook for 3 minutes. Mix in escarole, ham, ricotta, and egg. Season with pepper, nutmeg, and salt.
4. To Assemble: Cut dough into three pieces. Roll each on a floured board until 1/8-inch thick. Use a cutter to cut rounds. Place filling on lower half of each round, leaving a border. Fold top over filling and seal edges. Seal the tortelloni by pinching edges together.
5. For Sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan. Add cream and white pepper, whisking until boiling gently. Stir in pecorino cheese and tomato sauce, cooking 3 to 4 minutes.
6. Cook tortelloni in salted water until floating, then boil for 1 minute more. Drain and place in bowls with heated sauce. Serve immediately with additional Parmesan and white pepper.
Source: New York's Master Chefs, Bon Appetit Magazine
Written by Richard Sax, Photographs by Nancy McFarland
The Knapp Press, Los Angeles, 1985
Chef: Alfredo Viazzi, Cafe Domel Restaurant, New York
